content.text:
Based on my verification, here is my re-assessment.

**Verified resolved (not re-reported):**
- ✅ Finding 1 (`throw ex` stack-trace reset) — `TranslateException` removed entirely; replaced with `catch ... when (ex is ArgumentException or UnauthorizedAccessException or IOException)` so non-translated exceptions are never caught and propagate with original stack traces. Added 4 solid regression tests + a shared `AssertOriginalStackTracePreserved` helper. All 465 backend tests pass.
- ✅ Finding 3 (duplicated test helpers) — new `TestProject.Tests/TestHelpers/FileServiceTestFactory.cs` consolidates `CreateService`, `TryCreateDirectorySymlink`, `TryCreateFileSymlink`, and `FakeWebHostEnvironment`; both test classes now call it. Duplicates removed.
- ✅ Finding 2b/2c (stale refs in `copy-assets.test.ts` / `ignore-patterns.test.ts`) — removed.

**Not resolved / partial / new** are reported below.
final

Finding 2a (mock-response.ts still references the deleted characterization suite in src/api.test.ts) was NOT touched and remains misleading. Finding 4 (orphaned simple-git-hooks devDependency + lint-staged config) was NOT touched and remains dead. One minor new DRY nit was introduced in the new stack-trace tests (duplicated top-frame extraction).","findings":[{"id":"stale-docs-mock-response-characterization","severity":"medium","file":"src/test-utils/mock-response.ts:14-16","title":"mock-response.ts doc still claims coverage by a deleted characterization suite","description":"This pass resolved the stale references in copy-assets.test.ts and ignore-patterns.test.ts but left the third, most misleading one untouched. src/test-utils/mock-response.ts lines ~14-16 still end the doc block with: 'The behavior is pinned by the `mockResponse helper` characterization suite in `src/api.test.ts`.' That suite was removed during the refactor (verified: grep finds no 'characterization suite' / 'mockResponse helper' describe block in src/api.test.ts; mockResponse is only used there as an inline setup helper, never characterized). The claim is actively harmful: a maintainer reading the comment will believe mockResponse's edge branches (e.g. the json() fallback-to-empty-object path the comment itself flags as unreached) are regression-pinned when they are not. This is a stale-reference docs bug of exactly the kind flagged in the original review; it just wasn't fixed here.","fixPrompt":"In src/test-utils/mock-response.ts, edit the doc comment on the `mockResponse` function. Verify with `grep -rn \"characterization suite\" src` and `grep -rn \"mockResponse helper\" src` that no other stale reference to the deleted suite remains."},{"id":"orphaned-git-hook-tooling","severity":"low","file":"package.json:23-31","title":"simple-git-hooks devDependency + lint-staged config still orphaned (no trigger)","description":"Not addressed this pass. Confirmed: `grep` for prepare/simple-git-hooks/pre-commit across package.json and scripts/ finds only the devDependency line — nothing invokes or configures it. `simple-git-hooks` is now fully dead (no config, no script). The `lint-staged` config has lost its only automatic trigger; contributors will expect a pre-commit hook to run and it won't. This is leftover/orphaned tooling that should be cleaned up (or the wiring deliberately re-added).","fixPrompt":"In package.json: (1) remove `\"simple-git-hooks\": \"^2.13.1\"` from devDependencies — it has no config and no `prepare` script invoking it, so it is dead. (2) Remove the top-level `\"lint-staged\": { ... }` config block and the `\"lint-staged\": \"^16.4.0\"` devDependency, because its only trigger (the pre-commit hook that the removed `simple-git-hooks` wiring created) no longer exists. (NOTE: if the team intends to keep a manual `lint-staged` workflow, instead re-add a `\"prepare\": \"simple-git-hooks\"` script and the top-level `simple-git-hooks` config block re-wiring `pre-commit: npx lint-staged` so the existing lint-staged config actually runs on commit — do not leave the config without a trigger.) After editing, run `npm install` so package-lock.json drops the removed devDependency, then `npm run lint && npm test` to confirm nothing referenced the removed packages."},{"id":"duplicated-topframe-extraction-in-tests","severity":"low","file":"TestProject.Tests/Controllers/FilesControllerTests.cs:1010-1019,1037-1046","title":"Top-most stack-frame extraction duplicated across two new tests","description":"Regression introduced by this pass. The same pass already created a shared helper for the weaker assertion (`AssertOriginalStackTracePreserved`), so this stronger top-frame variant should be extracted the same way rather than copy-pasted into both tests. Minor DRY, but it directly contradicts the consolidation pattern the fixer just applied for finding 3.","fixPrompt":"In TestProject.Tests/Controllers/FilesControllerTests.cs, extract the duplicated top-most-frame logic into a new private static helper next to `AssertOriginalStackTracePreserved`, e.g. `private static void AssertTopFrameIs(Exception? thrown, string originMarker) { Assert.NotNull(thrown); var topFrame = thrown!.StackTrace!.Split(new[] { '\\r', '\\n' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries).Select(s => s.Trim()).FirstOrDefault(); Assert.NotNull(topFrame); Assert.Contains(originMarker, topFrame!); }`. Then replace the inline Split/Select/FirstOrDefault + Assert.NotNull + Assert.Contains blocks in both `PropagatedException_OriginalFrameIsTopMost_NotARethrownCopy_SyncEndpoint` and `_AsyncEndpoint` with a single call `AssertTopFrameIs(thrown, \"FakeFileService\")`, keeping the preceding `Assert.Same(ex, thrown)`.
